old_claim.note: Humanism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because scientific societies are scoped to organized natural-philosophy collaboration, correspondence, experiment, discussion, and publication rather than to humanist literary education.

source_shape.source_locator: Royal Society history says the new Fellowship concerned itself with natural philosophy, gathering information by correspondence, observing nature, conducting experiments, discussing outcomes, and publishing results.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports scientific collaboration and publication practices, not humanism as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Humanism remains adjacent intellectual culture; keeping it direct would make literary-humanist education a required edge for scientific society formation.

ontology_before: Scientific Societies depended directly on Humanism, implying humanist education was a causal prerequisite for the society form.
ontology_after: Scientific Societies depends on scientific method and universities; humanism remains adjacent intellectual context outside the direct prerequisite set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: humanism is no longer a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to Italian humanist academies rather than scientific societies.
A dependent needs scientific_societies to imply humanist literary culture.
A stronger source shows humanism directly caused the represented scientific society form.
short_reason: Humanism is context, not the society mechanism.
